title: Decomposition eval dataset example
description: Canonical contract for one decomposition eval dataset example. The repo owns this contract; Phoenix stores promoted example records that must conform to it. Examples preserve enough context for a human or model judge to decide whether the output is good; they do not require an exact golden issue split.

schema_version.description: Optional example contract version stamp. When present it must match this schema's $id.

project.description: Captured or sanitized Linear project snapshot. Never captured from live Linear state at promotion time.

content.description: Sanitized Linear project body. Subject to the field-level content allowlist/denylist; never token-shaped content.

status.description: Semantic project status at capture time.

pm.description: PM role runtime/model identity, for example codex/<model> or claude/<model>.

project_update_markdown.description: Exact authored project update. Required when terminal_status is completed or paused.

open_questions_markdown.description: Exact authored blockers prose. Required when terminal_status is paused.

human_annotations.description: Human judgments captured for this example. Empty until a human annotates. In MVP human-label authenticity is asserted, not authenticated, because local Phoenix is unauthenticated.

failure_modes.description: Failure mode ids from the versioned taxonomy in failure-taxonomy.json.

expected_properties.description: Property-level expectations a judge can check without an exact golden issue split.

metadata.description: Example metadata is Phoenix-bound: Agentic Factory workflow/queue state keys are banned (see $defs/no_workflow_state_keys and the wipe test in README.md); other extensibility stays open.

workspace_maturity.description: Human-set from the repo-owned workspace eval policy; no automatic transition logic in MVP.

lifecycle_state.description: active examples are eligible for default experiments and process-change gates; deprecated examples are retained for audit only.

dataset_split.description: Mirror of split membership for filtering, compatibility, or fallback reporting only. When the pinned Phoenix exposes native splits, native split membership wins; a mismatch is a reconciliation/evidence-quality problem, never silently merged.

source_trace_id.description: Originating trace id; null for synthetic or sanitized fixtures with no captured trace.

source_run_id.description: Originating run id; null for synthetic or sanitized fixtures with no captured run.

no_workflow_state_keys.description: Denylist for Phoenix-bound metadata: no Agentic Factory workflow/queue state may be stored in Phoenix (wipe test: losing Phoenix must never lose which behavior is accepted or what queue is pending). These known banned keys are schema-level rejections; other extensibility stays open.

quality_label.description: Default-judge label set. This set must not drift.

annotation_name.description: Canonical decomposition annotation names. decomposition_quality is the roll-up used for default gates; the narrower dimensions explain why the roll-up passed or failed; accepted_packet_sufficiency and pause_state_correctness are deterministic-check names.
